ops.action: Add or update a focused CLI UX test that asserts exit code and a small set of stable substrings/lines (not full output), covering both success and failure cases; avoid brittle assertions on whitespace, ordering, or environment-specific paths unless required.
ops.title: Add CLI UX regression tests with output changes
ops.trigger: When changing any CLI command behavior (stdout/stderr text, exit codes, help output, error messages)

ops.action: Update subsystem README/help docs alongside the CLI wiring, and factor logic into a testable core module so UX tests can stay thin; ensure docs mention common workflows and error modes, and add at least one UX test covering the new path.
ops.title: Keep docs + core aligned with new subcommands
ops.trigger: When introducing a new subcommand or expanding a subsystem CLI (e.g., `loom memory ...`)

ops.action: Treat the plugin as having a source-of-truth and a distributed copy: update both copies in the same change, or consolidate to a single source and add a deterministic sync step + a check (e.g., CI/test that asserts the two files are identical) so drift can’t happen silently.
ops.title: Keep distributed opencode plugin in sync
ops.trigger: When editing `.opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts` or any opencode plugin that is also shipped under `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/`

ops.body:
# Sync Distributed Opencode Plugin

Use this when a plugin exists in two places:
- `.opencode/plugins/<name>.ts` (dev copy)
- `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/<name>.ts` (shipped/distributed copy)

## Steps

1. Identify the two plugin paths that must match.
2. Make the change once, then immediately apply the exact same change to the other copy.
3. Verify they are identical (byte-for-byte) before moving on.
4. Add/keep a regression check that fails if the two files diverge.
5. Run repo gates: `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run basedpyright`, then the relevant tests.

## Guardrails

- Do not rely on memory that "it was copied earlier"; always diff the two files.
- Prefer a deterministic sync/check over human process when possible.
ops.description: Procedure to prevent drift between `.opencode/plugins/*` and the shipped copy under `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/*`.
ops.name: sync-distributed-opencode-plugin
